run_summary: Screen 2 has three flags across industrials and SaaS, all earnings within 3–6 trading days. One UNDERDONE flag (MOD) clears the BUY bar; two OVERDONE flags (MDB, ASAN) are structural SKIPs in this long-only book. No open positions to manage.

entries.name: MongoDB
entries.thesis: The combination of (a) explicit Atlas consumption slowdown language in the most recent 10-Q, (b) a brand-new CEO in his first full reporting quarter (high incentive to reset the bar), and (c) the sales-leader resignation announced 6 weeks before the print suggests elevated risk that the Q1 print and/or the FY27 guide come in below what the market currently embeds. Even if the headline beats, a new-CEO 'reset' on out-quarters is a recognized pattern.
entries.what_kills: AI workload wins drive an Atlas reacceleration, new CEO chooses to hold the line on guide rather than reset, or strong Voyage AI-related contract wins materially offset macro softness.
entries.reasoning: Classification is OVERDONE — long-only book has no way to express this structural short signal. OVERDONE flags are recorded as real Screen 2 signals (the filing evidence on Atlas consumption deceleration and CEO/sales-leader turnover is credible), but cannot be held as long positions in this portfolio.

entries.name: Asana
entries.thesis: Filings show retention deterioration (NRR below 100% on the highest-spend cohorts) coexisting with a heavy narrative pivot to AI/Agentic Enterprise that is not yet quantified as revenue contribution. Combined with executive departures and a disclosed security flaw in an AI-adjacent feature, the next print is more likely to surface continued NRR pressure and elevated AI investment than to deliver an upside catalyst — particularly if guidance was raised in Q3 on AI optimism.
entries.what_kills: NRR rebounds above 100%; a specific, quantified AI Studio/AI Teammates revenue contribution disclosure; clean reaffirmation of FY27 with strong large-customer growth (817 over-100K customers expanding).
entries.reasoning: Classification is OVERDONE — long-only book has no way to express this structural short signal. The filings evidence (NRR sub-100% on high-value cohorts, COO/GC departures, unproven AI pivot, disclosed security incident) is specific and well-grounded, but OVERDONE classification precludes a long position in this framework.
